metadata.purpose: Proofread transcript fixture for non-legacy E2E replay

entries.transcript_text: The Great Cake Caper
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: A Tale of Mice, Squirrels, and Questionable Decisions
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: A Testing Story
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Chapter One: A Very Good Idea
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: The afternoon sun filtered through the oak leaves as Kisten flicked his tail and stared across the park lawn.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: A family had spread a checkered blanket near the fountain, and right in the center of it sat the most magnificent cake he had ever seen—three layers, white frosting, dotted with strawberries.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: You’re drooling,
entries.speaker: Bitera

entries.transcript_text: said Bitera from beside him, her tiny pink nose twitching.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Kisten snapped his mouth shut.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: I am not. I was merely… assessing the situation.
entries.speaker: Kisten

entries.transcript_text: The situation is cake,
entries.speaker: Bitera

entries.transcript_text: Bitera said flatly.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: A slow, velvet-smooth voice came from behind them.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Don’t even think about it.
entries.speaker: Maddie

entries.transcript_text: Maddie dropped from the low branch above, landing without a sound.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: The tabby cat curled her tail around her paws and regarded them both with half-lidded amber eyes.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Those humans are wide awake.
entries.speaker: Maddie

entries.transcript_text: There are three of them,
entries.speaker: Kisten

entries.transcript_text: Kisten said, counting quietly.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: A mother, a father, and a very small child who appears to be mostly interested in grass.
entries.speaker: Kisten

entries.transcript_text: The child is a distraction,
entries.speaker: Bitera

entries.transcript_text: Bitera said, her dark eyes bright.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Maddie, you could walk right past them—they’ll think you’re a stray.
entries.speaker: Bitera

entries.transcript_text: Give them something to fuss over while Kisten and I do the work.
entries.speaker: Bitera

entries.transcript_text: Maddie’s ear flicked.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: You want me to be bait.
entries.speaker: Maddie

entries.transcript_text: I want you to be charming,
entries.speaker: Bitera

entries.transcript_text: Bitera corrected.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: You’re good at that.
entries.speaker: Bitera

entries.transcript_text: There was a long pause. Kisten watched Maddie’s gaze drift back toward the cake.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: He could tell the moment she made up her mind—her whiskers twitched just slightly.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: One slice,
entries.speaker: Maddie

entries.transcript_text: Maddie said.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: And I get the strawberry.
entries.speaker: Maddie

entries.transcript_text: Kisten grinned.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Deal.
entries.speaker: Kisten

entries.transcript_text: Bitera was already gone, slipping through the grass like a gray ribbon.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Kisten took a breath, checked the wind, and followed.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Chapter Two: The Great Caper
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: The plan, Kisten had to admit, was working almost too well.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Maddie had sauntered up to the picnic blanket with the easy confidence of someone who owned the entire park.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: The mother had said
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Oh!
entries.speaker: Mother

entries.transcript_text: and the father had said
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Come here, kitty,
entries.speaker: Father

entries.transcript_text: and the small child had shrieked with joy and lunged at Maddie’s tail.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Maddie bore all of this with tremendous dignity, which Kisten found privately impressive.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Now,
entries.speaker: Bitera

entries.transcript_text: Bitera whispered from beside him in the tall grass.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: They moved fast.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Bitera darted straight to the cake plate while Kisten circled wide, watching for danger.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: The cake sat on a paper plate at the very edge of the blanket.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Up close, it smelled of vanilla and sugar and everything wonderful in the world.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Can you lift it?
entries.speaker: Kisten

entries.transcript_text: he whispered.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Half a slice,
entries.speaker: Bitera

entries.transcript_text: Bitera said, already working at the edge of the frosting with quick, precise paws.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: More than enough.
entries.speaker: Bitera

entries.transcript_text: A shadow fell over them. Kisten froze.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: He turned very slowly to find the small child standing three feet away, staring at him with enormous brown eyes and a fistful of grass.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Nobody moved. A bee hummed past.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: The child sat down heavily in the grass and went back to examining a pebble.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Kisten let out a breath that felt like it had been stored since morning.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Go,
entries.speaker: Kisten

entries.transcript_text: he hissed.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: They ran.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Bitera clutched the chunk of cake against her chest, Kisten right behind her, both of them tearing across the lawn toward the oak tree where Maddie was already waiting, impeccably groomed, as if she had simply been sitting there all afternoon.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: The strawberry,
entries.speaker: Maddie

entries.transcript_text: she said, extending one paw.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: Bitera handed it over without argument.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: The three of them ate together in the shade, and Kisten decided it was,
entries.speaker: NARRATOR

entries.transcript_text: without question, the best cake he had ever tasted.
entries.speaker: NARRATOR
